# Verify Trend Simulation

## Claim being tested

A song, phrase, product, or person is naturally trending.

## Alternative hypothesis

A campaign seeded many signals to simulate momentum before organic uptake.

## Data fields

- account_id
- content_id
- audio_id
- timestamp
- caption
- hashtag sequence
- comment text
- source page / page category
- disclosure
- follower count
- engagement metrics

## Graphs

### Account-audio graph

Connect accounts to the audio or song they used.

### Account-account graph

Connect accounts that used the same audio with similar captions in tight time windows.

### Comment narrative graph

Connect comments with similar wording or repeated talking points.

## Metrics

- audio adoption curve
- first-wave concentration
- number of seed accounts before mainstream pickup
- caption entropy
- hashtag-sequence overlap
- centrality of pages that start the trend
- organic branching after first wave

## Key distinction

A real trend usually shows diverse second-order adoption. A simulated trend may show a strong seed wave but weak diversity outside campaign-linked accounts.

## Safe conclusion

"The early adoption pattern shows concentrated seeding consistent with trend-simulation dynamics; impact claims require downstream audience and conversion evidence."
